# Hover

> Open-source VS Code extension where AI drives your real browser to author end-to-end tests, then crystallises the session into a plain @playwright/test spec that runs in CI with zero AI and zero API keys.

Hover is a VS Code extension that puts a chat in your editor. You describe a flow in plain English ("log in, then add a todo"); an AI agent drives your actual Chrome over the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) via Playwright. When the run is verified, one click saves it as a standard `@playwright/test` spec under `__vibe_tests__/`. The saved spec runs in CI like any Playwright test — no agent, no model, no API key, no per-token billing. The extension drives your existing dev server over CDP, so nothing changes in your app or bundler config.

## What makes Hover different

- **AI authors once, then steps out.** The agent explores and writes the test during authoring; the committed artifact is plain deterministic Playwright code. CI never calls a model. Tools like Stagehand and Midscene keep a model in the loop at run time (every CI run costs tokens); Hover spends the model only once, at authoring.
- **BYO-CLI, no API key.** Hover bundles no AI runtime. It spawns whichever coding-agent CLI you already have on PATH — `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex` (OpenAI Codex) — riding the subscription you already pay for. There is no LLM SDK in Hover and nothing to authenticate.
- **The artifact is portable.** Output is standard `@playwright/test` code in your git repo. No proprietary format, no SaaS lock-in, no vendor dashboard required to run it.

## Install

Install the **Hover — AI E2E Testing & Security** extension from the VS Code Marketplace:

    https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=hyperyond.hover-dev

Or open the Extensions view in VS Code and search "Hover — AI E2E Testing & Security". You also need one coding-agent CLI on your PATH — Claude Code (`npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`) or OpenAI Codex (`npm i -g @openai/codex`) — signed in with your subscription or your own API key. The extension drives your existing dev server over CDP, so it works with any framework (Vite, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, webpack); native iOS / Android is out of scope.

## Key features

- Chat to a test file: describe a flow in plain English; Hover drives your real app and saves the verified run as a plain `@playwright/test` spec.
- Multi-environment accounts: define test accounts per environment, then mention `@account` in chat and the agent logs in. Credentials are parameterised into `process.env` references — never written into the spec — and export to CI secrets in one click.
- Specs / Sessions / Environments views, plus an optional off-by-default AI optimize pass you accept via diff.
- Re-record: when UI drift breaks a spec, the agent regenerates selectors from the original prompt.
- Security mode (🟠): a local HTTPS MITM lets the agent replay captured API calls with mutations to probe for IDOR, authz bypass, parameter tampering, missing security headers, and PII leakage. Confirmed findings crystallise into `.security.spec.ts` regression gates.
- Pentest mode (🔴): offensive vulnerability hunting (SQLi / XSS / SSTI / SSRF / open-redirect / IDOR) on your own dev app, producing a findings report. Authorized own-app testing only.

## Hover Cloud (planned, not yet available)

A future hosted service for running the specs you author locally: parallel hosted runs, scheduled monitoring with alerts, a dashboard with flakiness detection, PR checks, plus AI that works on results that already ran — self-healing re-record (regenerate selectors and open a PR when a spec breaks) and failure diagnosis. CI still runs plain Playwright; the AI only acts on what already broke, never on a green build. Authoring stays local and free. No launch date yet.

## Links

- Website: https://gethover.dev
- Documentation: https://gethover.dev/docs
- VS Code Marketplace: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=hyperyond.hover-dev
- Source (Apache-2.0): https://github.com/Hyperyond/Hover
